topics.intro.desc: Opening the conversation about enterprise AI development
topics.security.desc: Addressing security concerns and compliance requirements
topics.integration.desc: How Bob fits into existing workflows
topics.competition.desc: Differentiating from other AI coding tools

intro.title: Introduction to Bob
intro.subtitle: Opening the conversation about enterprise AI development
intro.q1: What is Bob, and how is it different from other AI coding tools like Copilot?

a1.p1: Great question. Bob is an enterprise-scale AI development platform that goes beyond code completion. While tools like Copilot help write individual functions, Bob orchestrates complex changes across your entire codebase.

diff1.title: Reads business requirements
diff1.desc: Upload a PDF spec, Bob understands what needs to be built

diff2.title: Multi-repo orchestration
diff2.desc: Coordinates changes across Java, Node.js, and React simultaneously

diff3.title: Compliance automation
diff3.desc: Generates SOC 2, PCI-DSS evidence as code is written

diff4.title: Security inline
diff4.desc: Catches vulnerabilities during generation, not days later

diff5.title: Full transparency
diff5.desc: Bobalytics shows exactly what you're spending and productivity gained

bottomline.label: Bottom line:
bottomline.text: We've seen 6,000+ IBM developers achieve 45% average productivity improvement. Projects that take 7-8 weeks traditionally get done in ~10 minutes with Bob.

productivity.title: Productivity Gains
productivity.subtitle: Quantifying the value proposition
productivity.q1: Those numbers sound too good to be true. 10 minutes vs 7-8 weeks? How is that possible?

a1.intro: I understand the skepticism. Let me break down where that time savings comes from:

week1.label: Week 1:
week1.text: Read business requirements, research existing systems

week2.label: Week 2:
week2.text: Map compliance requirements to technical implementation

withbob.title: With Bob (~10 minutes):
withbob.step1: Upload spec, Bob analyzes and identifies systems + gaps
withbob.step2: Bob maps SOC 2 findings to code implementation
withbob.step4: Bob generates production code with security built in
withbob.step5: Bob generates deployment runbook and compliance docs

guardium.name: IBM Guardium:
guardium.text: Completed work in 1 day that previously took 4 weeks (85% productivity gain)

security.title: Security & Compliance
security.subtitle: Addressing security concerns and compliance requirements
security.q1: We're in a regulated industry. How do we know Bob's generated code is secure? What about SOC 2 compliance?
security.q2: But what about data privacy? Does Bob send our code to external models?

builtin.title: Security: Built-in, Not Bolted-on
builtin.intro: Bob has shift-left security - vulnerabilities are caught during code generation, not in security reviews weeks later. Here's how:

realtime.title: Real-time scanning:
realtime.text: As Bob generates code, it simultaneously checks for vulnerabilities

autocorrect.title: Auto-correction:
autocorrect.text: If Bob generates code with a hardcoded secret, it self-corrects to use environment variables

prisma.title: Prisma AIRS integration:
prisma.text: AI-specific security scanning that catches issues traditional tools miss

zerodebt.title: Zero security debt:
zerodebt.text: No accumulation of vulnerabilities that need fixing later

compliance.title: Compliance: Automated Evidence Generation
compliance.intro: For SOC 2, PCI-DSS, HIPAA - Bob generates compliance evidence as it writes code:
compliance.point1: Upload audit findings → Bob maps each finding to specific code changes
compliance.point2: Bob generates the code AND the evidence you'll show auditors
compliance.point3: No last-minute scramble to gather documentation

proof.label: IBM Concert proof:
proof.text: Saved $84K per compliance project by automating this

keydiff.label: The key difference:
keydiff.text: Most AI tools help you write code. Bob helps you stay compliant while writing code.

a2.intro: Great question - this is critical for enterprises. Bob offers:

clientzero.title: Client-zero proof at IBM:
clientzero.text: 30,000 IBM developers use Bob daily on IBM's internal codebase

deployment.title: Deployment options:
deployment.text: Can run in your environment (Linux partitions on IBM systems with Spyre AI accelerator)

governance.title: Governance controls:
governance.text: Admin control over what code can be shared, model selection, cost limits per team

audit.title: Audit trail:
audit.text: Bobalytics shows every interaction for compliance tracking

a2.conclusion: We understand that for enterprise customers, data sovereignty and governance aren't features - they're requirements.

multirepo.title: Multi-Repository Orchestration
multirepo.subtitle: Handling complex, distributed codebases
multirepo.q1: We have microservices across 20+ repositories. Can Bob really handle that complexity?

a1.yes: Yes - this is exactly what Bob was built for.
a1.intro: Most AI coding tools work on single files or single repos. They can't understand that a change in your auth-service requires updates to your user-service (Java), frontend (React), and API gateway.

example.title: Real Example: SSO + MFA Implementation

task.label: The task:
task.text: Add SSO and MFA across 3 systems to pass SOC 2 audit

example.step2: Bob identifies all 3 affected repos: auth-service (Node.js), user-service (Java), frontend (React)
example.step3: Bob creates implementation plan with deployment order (auth → user → frontend)
example.step4: Bob generates 17+ files across all 3 repos with proper integration points
example.step5: Bob includes API contracts between services

time.label: Time:
time.text: 10 minutes. Traditional approach: 7-8 weeks with coordination across teams.

conclusion.label: For your 20+ repos:
conclusion.text: Bob scales because it maintains context across your entire codebase, not just the files currently open in your IDE.

cost.title: Cost Transparency
cost.subtitle: Bobalytics and ROI visibility
cost.q1: AI tools can get expensive quickly. How do we track costs? My CFO will ask for ROI justification.

bobalytics.title: Bobalytics is unique to Bob
bobalytics.intro: no other AI tool provides this level of transparency:

tasklevel.title: Task-level costing:
tasklevel.text: See exactly what each task cost (e.g., "$8.47 for SSO implementation")

dashboards.title: Team dashboards:
dashboards.text: Leadership can see productivity metrics, cost per team, usage patterns

budget.title: Budget controls:
budget.text: Set spending limits per team or project

roi.title: ROI calculator:
roi.text: Compare traditional cost vs Bob cost for every project

cfo.label: Your CFO gets:
cfo.point2: Productivity metrics (45% average improvement across 6,000 IBM devs)

a1.conclusion: No other AI tool gives you this level of visibility into what you're spending and what you're getting for it.

integration.title: Integration & Deployment
integration.subtitle: How Bob fits into existing workflows
integration.q1: What tools does Bob integrate with? We use GitHub, Jenkins, and have strict deployment processes.

a1.intro: Bob is designed to fit into enterprise workflows, not replace them:

cicd.title: CI/CD:
cicd.text: Jenkins, GitLab CI, GitHub Actions, Tekton

workflow.title: How it works in your workflow:
workflow.step1: Developer uses Bob in their IDE to generate code
workflow.step2: Code goes through your normal PR review process
workflow.step3: Bob integrates with your CI/CD pipeline for testing
workflow.step4: Deploy using your existing tools and processes

a1.governance: Bob doesn't bypass your governance - it accelerates within it.

bonus.title: Bonus: Deployment Planning
bonus.text: Bob also generates deployment runbooks with zero-downtime strategies and rollback procedures - tailored to your infrastructure.

competition.title: vs Competitors
competition.subtitle: Differentiating from other AI coding tools
competition.q1: We're already using Copilot. Why would we add Bob?

copilot.intro: Copilot is great for individual developer productivity.
copilot.text: If your goal is to help developers write functions faster, Copilot works well.

both.label: Many IBM teams use both:
both.text: Copilot for daily coding, Bob for the complex enterprise initiatives that span teams and require compliance oversight.

unique.title: Unique to Bob - Copilot Can't Do:
unique.point1: Read a 20-page business requirements PDF and map to code
unique.point3: Generate SOC 2 audit evidence as code is written
unique.point4: Show you exactly what you're spending (Bobalytics)
unique.point5: Catch security vulnerabilities during generation (not after)

proof.title: Proof Points
proof.subtitle: Real-world evidence and case studies
proof.q1: This all sounds great, but who's actually using this in production? I need real references.

clientzero.title: Client-Zero Proof at IBM
clientzero.intro: Before any external customer, Bob was battle-tested inside IBM:

result.label: Result:
result.text: 85% productivity gain - completed in 1 day what previously took 4 weeks

result.label: Result:
result.text: Saved $84,000 per compliance project by automating evidence generation

result.label: Result:
result.text: Accelerated feature delivery across distributed teams

references.label: For references:
references.text: We can connect you with IBM product teams who are using Bob in production. They can speak directly to the productivity gains and how they've integrated it into their workflows.

cta.title: Ready to Continue the Conversation?
cta.subtitle: Explore Bob's capabilities hands-on or dive deeper into any topic
